---
title: "Quest 9: Sorting - Organizing Data"
subtitle: "Sort Vectors with Built-in Methods"
format:
  live-html:
    code-tools: true
---

::: {.quest-badge}
📊 QUEST 9 | Difficulty: Beginner | Time: 8 minutes
:::

::: {.tip-box}
**💻 Practice Options:**
- 🦀 [Open Rust Playground](https://play.rust-lang.org/)
- 📓 [View Notebook](../files/lessons/09-sorting.ipynb)
- 📥 [Download Notebook](../files/lessons/09-sorting.ipynb)
:::

## 📖 Introduction
Sorting helps you present data clearly and search faster.
Take it one step at a time—you've got this.

## 💡 Rust Sorting

```rust
fn main() {
    let mut numbers = vec![9, 2, 7, 1, 5];
    numbers.sort();
    println!("Ascending: {:?}", numbers);

    numbers.sort_by(|a, b| b.cmp(a));
    println!("Descending: {:?}", numbers);
}
```

## 🎯 Gentle Challenge
Sort a list of 6 test scores from low to high, then print the top score.
Try changing one number and run again to confirm the order updates.

## ✅ One Possible Solution

```rust
fn main() {
    let mut scores = vec![88, 72, 95, 67, 91, 84];
    scores.sort();
    println!("Sorted: {:?}", scores);

    if let Some(top) = scores.last() {
        println!("Top score: {}", top);
    }
}
```

::: {.quest-complete}
### ✨ Quest 9 Complete
You used Rust sorting tools on vectors.
Next: [Quest 10: Number Game](10-number-game.qmd)
:::
